summary: Patient Data Encryption and Audit Trails Test Case
description: This test case validates that patient data is encrypted using AES-256 and audit trails are stored for every database change.
objective: Verify that the system encrypts patient data using AES-256 and stores audit trails as per requirement.
prerequisites: The system must be configured to use AES-256 encryption
Audit trail settings must be enabled
test_steps: Step 1: Enter a new patient record
Step 2: Inspect the stored data to verify it is encrypted with AES-256
Step 3: Trigger a database change (e.g. update or delete a record)
Step 4: Observe the system response and verify that an audit trail is created
expected_result: The system must encrypt patient data using AES-256 and store an audit trail for every database change.
test_data: Sample patient data (e.g. name, date of birth, medical history)
acceptance_criteria: Patient data is encrypted with AES-256
Audit trails are stored for every database change
